name: Moussaka (Lamb and Eggplant Casserole)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove green caps from eggplants and slice them in half lengthwise. Make deep slashes in the eggplant pulp, but do not cut through the skins. Sprinkle the eggplant halves with 1 tablespoon salt and allow to stand at room temperature for 30 minutes.
2. Squeeze moisture out of the eggplant halves and brush the cut surfaces with 2 tablespoons olive oil. Heat 4 eggplant halves at a time in the microwave for 7 minutes or until tender. Repeat with remaining eggplant halves.
3. Scoop pulp out of the eggplants, being careful not to rip the skins. Set the skins aside. Chop the eggplant pulp coarsely. Place it in a medium-sized, heat-resistant, non-metallic bowl and heat, uncovered, for 4 minutes or until tender. Stir occasionally.
4. In a large heat-resistant, non-metallic bowl, crumble the lamb. Heat, uncovered, for 5 minutes stirring frequently to break up pieces until the meat is no longer pink.
5. Liberally oil a deep, 2-quart, heat-resistant, non-metallic casserole dish. Line the casserole with the reserved eggplant skins, arranging them with the purple sides toward the outside and wide ends of the eggplant skins at the top of the casserole.
6. Drain the lamb juices and discard. Add chopped eggplant, onion, mushrooms, 1 teaspoon salt, thyme, rosemary, and garlic to the cooked lamb. Stir to combine well. Heat, uncovered, for 5 minutes or until the onion is tender.
7. In a small heat-resistant, non-metallic bowl, combine beef stock and cornstarch until smooth. Heat, uncovered, for 1 minute or until thickened and clear; stir occasionally.
8. Add the thickened beef stock with remaining ingredients (except tomato sauce) to the lamb mixture. Pour the lamb-eggplant mixture into the eggplant-skin-lined casserole.
9. Fold the eggplant skins over the filling. Heat, covered with a plate, for 9 minutes or until a knife inserted in the center of the mixture comes out clean.
10. Invert onto a platter for serving. Serve with tomato sauce.
